agency_cn: 国家自然科学基金委员会
description: National Natural Science Foundation of China proposal scoring rubric. NOTE: NSFC does not publish official numerical weights — these weights are estimated based on common reviewer practice and should be treated as approximate guidelines. Evaluates proposals across four dimensions: innovation, feasibility, PI capability, and value. Bilingual (Chinese/English) sub-criteria.
funding_constraint_note: NSFC funding rates are highly competitive. Reviewers should be aware of annual budget constraints and prioritize proposals with the highest combined innovation and value scores.

overall_scoring.pass_fail_rule: Proposals scoring below 3.5 overall or below 3.0 on any single dimension are not recommended for funding.
overall_scoring.description: Overall score is the weighted average of the four dimension scores. Both the overall threshold and per-dimension minimum must be met.

innovation.name: Innovation
innovation.description: The originality and novelty of the proposed research in terms of concepts, methods, and cross-disciplinary integration.

sub_criteria.name: Concept Novelty
sub_criteria.description: The originality of the core scientific ideas, hypotheses, or theoretical framework proposed.

score_descriptors.1: No novelty. Ideas are entirely derivative or previously published by others.
score_descriptors.2: Minimal novelty. Minor variations on well-established concepts with little original thought.
score_descriptors.3: Moderate novelty. Some original ideas but largely rooted in existing frameworks.
score_descriptors.4: High novelty. Clearly original concepts that offer new perspectives or challenge existing views.
score_descriptors.5: Exceptional novelty. Groundbreaking ideas with the potential to open entirely new research directions.

sub_criteria.name: Approach Novelty
sub_criteria.description: The originality and inventiveness of the proposed research methods, techniques, or experimental designs.

score_descriptors.1: Standard methods only. No methodological contribution whatsoever.
score_descriptors.2: Mostly standard methods with trivial modifications.
score_descriptors.3: Some novel methodological elements combined with established techniques.
score_descriptors.4: Innovative methods that represent clear advances over current practice.
score_descriptors.5: Pioneering methodology that could become a new standard in the field.

sub_criteria.name: Cross-Disciplinary Integration
sub_criteria.description: The degree to which the proposal meaningfully integrates knowledge, methods, or perspectives from multiple disciplines.

score_descriptors.1: No cross-disciplinary elements. Work is confined to a single narrow sub-field.
score_descriptors.2: Superficial mention of other fields without meaningful integration.
score_descriptors.3: Some cross-disciplinary elements that add modest value to the research design.
score_descriptors.4: Strong interdisciplinary integration that substantively enriches the research approach.
score_descriptors.5: Exceptional cross-disciplinary synthesis that creates transformative synergies and could not be achieved within a single discipline.

feasibility.name: Feasibility
feasibility.description: The likelihood that the proposed research can be successfully completed as designed, considering methodology, data, timeline, and risk management.

sub_criteria.name: Methodology Rigor
sub_criteria.description: The soundness, appropriateness, and rigor of the proposed research methodology and analytical framework.

score_descriptors.1: Methods are absent or fundamentally flawed. Cannot produce valid results.
score_descriptors.2: Methods have major weaknesses. Key steps are poorly justified or missing.
score_descriptors.3: Methods are generally appropriate but have notable gaps or insufficient justification.
score_descriptors.4: Methods are rigorous and well-justified with only minor areas needing refinement.
score_descriptors.5: Methods are exemplary, representing best-in-class rigor with thorough justification for all choices.

sub_criteria.name: Preliminary Data
sub_criteria.description: The quality, relevance, and completeness of preliminary results or pilot studies that support the proposed research.

score_descriptors.1: No preliminary data. No evidence of prior work in this direction.
score_descriptors.2: Weak or tangentially related preliminary data. Does not convincingly support the proposal.
score_descriptors.3: Some relevant preliminary data but with gaps. Partially supports the feasibility.
score_descriptors.4: Strong preliminary data that clearly demonstrates the viability of the proposed approach.
score_descriptors.5: Comprehensive and compelling preliminary data providing robust evidence for feasibility and likely success.

sub_criteria.name: Timeline Realism
sub_criteria.description: Whether the proposed project timeline and milestones are realistic given the scope of work and available resources.

score_descriptors.1: Timeline is completely unrealistic. Impossible to complete as described.
score_descriptors.2: Timeline is overly optimistic with critical tasks underestimated.
score_descriptors.3: Timeline is somewhat realistic but key phases may be too compressed.
score_descriptors.4: Timeline is realistic with well-defined milestones and reasonable buffer time.
score_descriptors.5: Timeline is carefully planned with clear milestones, dependencies, and appropriate contingency margins.

sub_criteria.name: Risk Mitigation
sub_criteria.description: The identification of potential risks and the quality of proposed mitigation strategies and alternative plans.

score_descriptors.1: No risk assessment. Potential problems are entirely ignored.
score_descriptors.2: Risks are acknowledged superficially with no concrete mitigation strategies.
score_descriptors.3: Some risks identified with partial mitigation plans. Key risks may be overlooked.
score_descriptors.4: Good risk assessment with concrete and credible mitigation strategies for most identified risks.
score_descriptors.5: Thorough risk analysis with well-developed contingency plans and alternative approaches for all significant risks.

pi_capability.name: PI Capability
pi_capability.description: The qualifications, experience, and leadership ability of the principal investigator and the research team.

sub_criteria.name: Track Record
sub_criteria.description: The PI's past research accomplishments, including publications, grants, awards, and demonstrated ability to complete funded projects.

score_descriptors.1: No relevant track record. PI has not demonstrated ability in this research area.
score_descriptors.2: Limited track record. Few relevant publications or completed projects.
score_descriptors.3: Moderate track record. Some relevant publications and completed projects but not leading the field.
score_descriptors.4: Strong track record. Substantial publications in relevant venues and successful project completion.
score_descriptors.5: Outstanding track record. Leading researcher with high-impact publications, successful prior grants, and recognized expertise.

sub_criteria.name: Team Composition
sub_criteria.description: The appropriateness of the research team's composition, including complementary expertise, roles, and collaborative arrangements.

score_descriptors.1: Team is missing critical expertise. Roles are undefined or inappropriate.
score_descriptors.2: Team has significant gaps in expertise. Collaboration structure is poorly defined.
score_descriptors.3: Team is adequate but could benefit from additional expertise. Roles are generally clear.
score_descriptors.4: Well-composed team with complementary skills and clearly defined roles and responsibilities.
score_descriptors.5: Exceptional team with optimal expertise coverage, strong collaborative history, and well-structured roles.

sub_criteria.name: Publication Quality
sub_criteria.description: The quality and impact of the PI's and team's publication record, assessed by venue prestige, citation impact, and relevance.

score_descriptors.1: No relevant publications or only publications in non-peer-reviewed outlets.
score_descriptors.2: Few relevant publications, mostly in lower-tier journals with limited citations.
score_descriptors.3: Reasonable publication record in solid peer-reviewed journals. Moderate citation impact.
score_descriptors.4: Strong publication record in high-quality journals with good citation impact.
score_descriptors.5: Exceptional publications in top-tier journals with high citation impact and field-defining contributions.

value.name: Value
value.description: The potential academic, societal, and talent development contributions of the proposed research.

sub_criteria.name: Academic Contribution
sub_criteria.description: The potential for the research to advance scientific knowledge, create new theories, or resolve important open questions.

score_descriptors.1: No discernible academic contribution. Results would not advance the field.
score_descriptors.2: Minimal academic contribution. Incremental results unlikely to attract significant attention.
score_descriptors.3: Moderate academic contribution. Useful results that would be of interest to specialists.
score_descriptors.4: Significant academic contribution. Results would advance the field and attract broad interest.
score_descriptors.5: Major academic contribution. Results could redefine understanding in the field and generate widespread impact.

sub_criteria.name: Societal Impact
sub_criteria.description: The potential for the research to produce practical benefits for society, economy, public health, or national development.

score_descriptors.1: No societal impact. Research is purely academic with no foreseeable application.
score_descriptors.2: Negligible societal impact. Potential applications are speculative or very distant.
score_descriptors.3: Some societal relevance. Potential for practical application in the medium to long term.
score_descriptors.4: Clear societal impact. Research addresses real-world problems with a credible path to application.
score_descriptors.5: High societal impact. Research directly addresses urgent national or global challenges with strong application potential.

sub_criteria.name: Talent Development
sub_criteria.description: The project's potential to train and develop the next generation of researchers, including graduate students and postdoctoral fellows.

score_descriptors.1: No talent development component. No students or junior researchers involved.
score_descriptors.2: Minimal talent development. Students are involved but with no structured training plan.
score_descriptors.3: Moderate talent development. Some training activities planned for students and junior researchers.
score_descriptors.4: Strong talent development. Clear training plan with meaningful research experience for students and postdocs.
score_descriptors.5: Exceptional talent development. Comprehensive mentoring program with structured career development and multiple training opportunities.
